title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple (2026) - Film Overview
text: Directed by Nia DaCosta and written by Alex Garland, this horror-thriller sequel released January 2026 with the tagline Fear is the new faith. The narrative follows two parallel storylines: Dr. Kelson enters an unexpected relationship with world-altering consequences, while Spike faces a terrifying encounter with Jimmy Crystal that haunts him. Key Cast: Ralph Fiennes (Dr. Kelson), Jack O'Connell (Jimmy Crystal), Alfie Williams (Spike). Production Team: Producers include Danny Boyle and Alex Garland; composer Hildur Guðnadóttir returns for the score. Reviewers highlight Fiennes' standout performance, with one noting: how many more perfect performances must Ralph Fiennes give us before we give him an Oscar. The film balances visceral horror with dark comedy, earning predominantly four-star ratings from critics. One reviewer observed the film maintains a sick sense of humor despite its brutality, while another praised its thematic richness within the trilogy. Runtime: 109 minutes. Genres: Horror, Science Fiction, Thriller. Fan Base: 3.2K fans on Letterboxd. Theatrical release spans globally January 14-16, 2026, with digital and physical releases planned for spring 2026.
